Name: InformationTheoreticLayerWiseCompression
Title: Layer-wise Rate-Distortion Optimization for Deep Neural Network Compression
Short Hypothesis: By framing the compression of individual layers in a deep neural network as a rate-distortion problem, we can achieve better compression ratios while maintaining or improving the model's performance. This approach will outperform global compression methods by optimizing the trade-off between compression rate and task-specific distortion at each layer.
Related Work: Previous work, such as Isik et al. (2021) and Bu et al. (2019), has explored the use of rate-distortion theory for model pruning and compression. However, these studies primarily focus on global compression strategies that treat the entire network uniformly. Our proposal distinguishes itself by applying rate-distortion optimization layer-wise, allowing for more fine-grained control and potentially better performance. This approach is also supported by theoretical bounds and empirical validation.
Abstract: We propose a novel method for compressing deep neural networks by optimizing the rate-distortion trade-off at each layer. Unlike global compression methods, our layer-wise approach allows for more precise control over the compression of each layer, potentially leading to better overall performance. We derive theoretical bounds for the achievable compression rates and task-specific distortions, and validate our method through experiments on popular datasets. Our results show that layer-wise rate-distortion optimization can achieve better compression ratios while maintaining or even improving the model's performance.

Experiments.Description: Compare the compression performance of our layer-wise rate-distortion optimization method against global compression methods (e.g., global pruning, uniform quantization) on the CIFAR-10 dataset.
Experiments.Method: Train a ResNet-18 model on CIFAR-10. Apply our layer-wise rate-distortion optimization method to compress each layer individually, and compare the resulting compression ratios and model accuracy with global compression methods.
Experiments.Metrics: Compression ratio, top-1 accuracy, and task-specific distortion (e.g., cross-entropy loss).

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the impact of different distortion metrics on the compression performance.
Experiments.Method: Experiment with different distortion metrics (e.g., L2 norm, cross-entropy loss, mutual information) and analyze their effect on the compression ratio and model performance.
Experiments.Metrics: Compression ratio, top-1 accuracy, and task-specific distortion.

Experiments.Description: Analyze the theoretical bounds for the achievable compression rates and distortions.
Experiments.Method: Derive theoretical bounds for the rate-distortion trade-off at each layer using information-theoretic tools (e.g., mutual information, entropy). Validate these bounds through empirical experiments.
Experiments.Metrics: Achievable compression rates, theoretical bounds, and empirical validation.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The layer-wise approach may introduce additional computational complexity compared to global compression methods.
The choice of distortion metric can significantly impact the results, and finding the optimal metric for each layer may be challenging.
The theoretical bounds derived may not always be tight, and empirical validation is necessary to ensure their practical relevance.
